text: Entering this clean, modern space, I feel a quiet anticipation. The glass walls suggest transparency and openness, yet the tilted angle of my view evokes a slight disorientation—a reminder that perception is always subjective. Stepping inside the exhibit, I am greeted by vibrant colors and words about research and development. I feel intellectually stimulated, as if standing at the crossroads of curiosity and innovation. The displays invite me to contemplate the relationship between humans and technology, leaving me reflective, eager to ponder the progression of knowledge and my own role in it.
